main_event: Civilian druggist in Waverly, Illinois observed object larger than an airplane, white and green with fuzzy edges, moving north, for 20 minutes on night of 8-9 November 1957; 15 other people reportedly saw same object; teletype report was garbled and could not be evaluated; second report describes large round reddish-green stationary object.
sighted_object: larger than an airplane; white and green (first sighting) / large round very bright reddish green (second sighting); edges fuzzy or blurred; no tail or exhaust; no sound; appeared solid; observed through binoculars
witness_description: Primary witness: Mr. [REDACTED], Waverly, Illinois, age unknown, druggist; 15 other people reportedly saw same object, information not given; second report: Mr. [REDACTED], Waverly, Ill., age unknown
conclusion: Other (Garbled MSc) / Insufficient Data for Evaluation
interesting_points: File contains TWO record cards and TWO teletype reports representing potentially separate sightings
Card 1 (8 Nov 57, 09/0015Z): White and green object moving north, 20 minutes — record card conclusion: 'Other - garbled' because teletype was garbled and could not be evaluated

Card 2 (9 Nov 57): Large round reddish-green stationary object — conclusion: Insufficient Data for Evaluation; comments note reddish green stationary characteristics are characteristic of Venus but insufficient info
Second teletype (28th Air Div, 12/0224Z): Conditions 'IMP. DATA MISSING'; normal traffic in division area
ATIC handwritten comment: 'Venus now so bright papers have reported on its brilliance, but cannot conclude this was [Venus]'
CSV flag B:witnesses_16_not_in_text: haiku correctly derived 16 (15 others + primary) from '15 other people saw same object'; validator did not find literal '16' in text
